title: Cheapguide
summary: Served as Principal Backend Engineer & Solution Architect in a 4-person cross-functional team, architecting and shipping a Spring Boot backend with REST APIs for auth, posts, search, AI moderation, and notifications
highlights: Integrated Firebase Auth (JWT) with role-based access control (RBAC), enforcing authorisation across 30+ protected endpoints serving 3 user roles
Researched and proposed replacing a Redis sidecar with Caffeine in-memory caching, eliminating ~$400/year in infrastructure overhead while achieving sub-millisecond data retrieval
Set up async workflows with Google Pub/Sub (handling 16 concurrent threads) and provisioned GCP infrastructure via Terraform, reducing deployment time with verifiable Infrastructure-as-Code
Established CI/CD with GitHub Actions utilising multi-stage Docker builds (reducing image size by ~40%) and ensured production monitoring with OpenTelemetry tracing and structured logging

title: ParkPie
summary: Led SDLC as AI & Backend Lead in a 6-person Agile team, architecting a geospatial-first Django backend leveraging AWS EC2, RDS (PostGIS), Cognito auth, and S3 for secure uploads
highlights: Designed a domain-driven PostgreSQL schema managing 1.9M+ geospatial records, separating runtime map state from immutable temporal history to ensure auditable data provenance via CQRS-inspired REST APIs
Pioneered an agentic AI pipeline with LangGraph and LangChain, combining a hybrid Gemini/Ollama inference model with PGVector semantic search for reliable Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Engineered a DevSecOps CI/CD pipeline in GitHub Actions, enforcing Test-driven development across 350+ automated tests, Mypy strict type-checking, Bandit security scanning, and Docker builds to GHCR
Added comprehensive monitoring and tracing with LangSmith for LLM behaviour and OpenTelemetry + the LGTM stack (Loki, Grafana, Tempo, Mimir) to observe complex agentic workflows
